titleStmt.title: Letter from W.D. O’Kelly to E. O’Farrell, 9 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W.D. O’Kelly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Declan O'Kelly (d. 1968) to Sir Edward O’Farrell (c.1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary. In this letter O'Kelly responds to the assistant under-secretary's queries on details of a Return of Experiments previously submitted for the year 1915.William Declan O'Kelly was a pathologist at the Department of Pathology and Bacteriology at UCD. After the Irish Civil War O'Kelly carried out the post-mortem examination on the remains of Noel Lemass. He subsequently served for a time as the Irish representative to the World. Health Organisation.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/4440

correspAction.placeName: Department of Pathology and Bacteriology, School of Medicine, Cecelia Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
University College, Dublin Department of Pathology and Bacteriology, School of Medicine, Cecilia Street, Dublin. 9.3.'16
Your Ref. No. 3500. Sir, With reference to your enquiry enclosed, the number in table 2 should
be 106, not 124. I have altered the number in table 2 and regret
having overlooked the discrepancy when returning the form. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient servant, W.D. O'Kelly
